goal_summary: Use one clean 7-day cycle to learn what event Wendy is planning and, if there is real interest, turn that clarity into a scheduled phone call with Andre.
lead_state_summary: Civic Arts Collective — Wendy Quinn is a new Potential lead created on May 2 with one email and one phone number on file. Andre already sent a same-day SMS and email on May 3 asking what kind of event they are planning, then sent another email on May 4, and two very similar SMS attempts on May 5 noting he did not want to crowd her and asking what event she is planning. There is no recorded reply, no workflow enrollment, and no evidence yet of event type, guest count, date, budget, or decision process.
known_facts: Lead name is Civic Arts Collective — Wendy Quinn.
Wendy Quinn has an email address and a mobile number on file.
Andre sent first-touch outreach on 2026-05-03 by both SMS and email.
Andre sent two similar SMS messages on 2026-05-05 asking what event Civic Arts Collective is planning.
No inbound reply is recorded in the Box.
There are no workflow enrollments to account for.
unknowns: What event Civic Arts Collective is planning
Whether they are still actively looking
Whether Wendy prefers text, email, or a phone call
best_next_question: Wendy, when Civic Arts Collective starts looking at catering, what usually needs to be true before it makes sense to have a quick call?

days.objective: Pause active outreach today to avoid crowding after multiple recent SMS attempts and prepare a cleaner next move.

required_actions.draft_seed: Do not send another live touch today. Note that two similar SMS messages went out on May 5 with no reply, so the next outbound should be a reset, not another event-type ask.
required_actions.notes: Last outbound was SMS today; respect rolling 24h cap and reduce pressure.

days.send_window: No send today; internal review only

days.objective: Reset the conversation with one low-pressure SMS that acknowledges the silence and invites the easiest next step.

required_actions.draft_seed: Wendy, I may be reaching at a busy time. Is catering for Civic Arts Collective something you're still sorting out, or should I close the loop on my side?
required_actions.notes: Short, permission-based reset. Different intent from prior asks.

days.objective: Create a call path if Wendy engages and otherwise prepare context-rich follow-up.

required_actions.draft_seed: If Wendy replies with interest, respond by answering her point first and offer two short call windows with Andre. If she says not now, ask if there is a better month to reconnect. If no reply, keep day 4 email focused on helping her decide whether a call is worth it.
required_actions.notes: Internal branching task; no outbound unless she replies.

days.send_window: Review between 9:00 AM - 5:00 PM lead-local time

days.objective: Send one concise email that makes replying easy and frames the call as a fit check, not a pitch.

required_actions.draft_seed:
Subject: Is this for an upcoming Civic Arts Collective event?

Wendy — I haven't been able to tell whether this is for a fundraiser, staff gathering, opening, or something else.

Would it be easier to reply with:
- event type
- rough headcount
- target date

If a quick call is simpler, I can have Andre hold 10 minutes to see if we're even the right fit.
required_actions.notes: Use specificity around likely arts-org event types to show relevance without guessing too hard.

days.objective: Make one light phone attempt and leave a voicemail only if it feels appropriate.

required_actions.draft_seed: Hi Wendy, this is Andre with Comeketo. I’ve reached out a couple of times because I couldn’t tell what kind of event Civic Arts Collective is planning. If catering is still on your list, a quick 10-minute call can tell us whether it makes sense to keep talking. You can text me back with the event type if that's easier.
required_actions.notes: If no answer, leave brief voicemail once. Do not call twice.

days.objective: Use a final SMS to ask a decision-driving question that opens the door to a scheduled call.

required_actions.draft_seed: Wendy, when your team looks at catering, what usually needs to be clear before a short call is worth having?
required_actions.notes: NEPQ-style question aimed at surfacing process and friction.

days.objective: Close the loop respectfully by email and invite either a call or a no-for-now response.

required_actions.draft_seed:
Subject: Should I keep this open?

Wendy — I haven’t heard back, so I don’t want to keep filling your inbox.

If Civic Arts Collective is still planning an event, send me the date and headcount and I’ll let you know whether a short call with Andre makes sense.

If this is no longer on your plate, you can simply reply "close it" and I’ll do that.
required_actions.notes: Respectful close that still gives a clear path to re-engage.

stop_conditions.trigger: Lead replies with stop, remove me, unsubscribe, or not interested
Lead replies with event details or asks a question
Lead agrees to a phone conversation or asks for availability
Lead requests a quote before a call
